title: Add a latent tyre-state sidecar instead of more hand-built degradation formulas
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than a directly observed scalar.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than a directly observed scalar. That fits this dataset: TyreLife, Cumulative_Degradation, and LapTime_Delta are noisy proxies for the unobserved tyre condition that actually drives pit calls.
implementation_hint: Train a small sidecar model that predicts a latent 'stint health' score or next-lap degradation regime from current and lagged per-driver stint variables. Use its out-of-fold predictions as features for the classifier rather than directly hard-coding more window equations.
expected_effect: Should separate 'old but still stable' stints from true cliff-edge stints, especially across compounds and circuits.
risk: Needs strictly causal lags and out-of-fold generation; otherwise the sidecar can quietly leak future stint information.
